summary: Ukrainian FPV drone units, including the SBU's "Alpha" unit, have achieved significant operational success, reportedly neutralizing 1,834 Russian forces over a week, while the Ukrainian Navy used FPV drones to sink an enemy boat near Tendrivska Spit in Kherson Oblast. Russian Ka-52 helicopters have been downed by Ukrainian FPV drones on multiple occasions, including near Nadiivka in Donetsk region, where crews were eliminated after crash landings. Analysts note that while analog FPV systems remain dominant due to reliability in signal loss, digital systems are emerging for anti-drone defenses. Russian forces have suffered losses from coordinated FPV strikes, including simultaneous hits on infantry and novel tactics like charging drones with bayonets. Meanwhile, Russian systems like "Rubikon" and "Elka" have intercepted Ukrainian drones, with Rubikon claiming 83 targets destroyed in a single day and Elka achieving kinetic interception of a Ukrainian "Darts" drone.
